content:
- Verified the remote Publish GHCR Image run completed successfully for upgrade commit 43c18e3ec9d986e55525d1457412699627ea94f6 and verified the SHA-tagged GHCR image via docker buildx imagetools inspect.
- Marked the task and plan complete, then ran .ralph/task_switch.sh which updated current_tasks/current_tasks_done and sent the completion email.
- Next: create the required final task-finished commit with all remaining .ralph/task bookkeeping, push it, and quit immediately.

- Completed story-08 task-03: upgraded go.mod and Dockerfile to Go 1.26.2, passed make check/lint/test and docker build locally, and verified the remote GHCR workflow for upgrade commit 43c18e3ec9d986e55525d1457412699627ea94f6.
- Verified ghcr.io/djosh34/pg_gobench:43c18e3ec9d986e55525d1457412699627ea94f6 exists as a multi-platform image via docker buildx imagetools inspect; then marked the task done, ran task_switch, created final commit 65fa752, and pushed it.
- Next after reopening: no follow-up required for this task unless someone wants to inspect the subsequent bookkeeping-only push workflow.

- Read the Choose-Task instructions, prior progress log, and the list-active-tasks skill.
- Refreshed Ralph task summaries with .ralph/update_task_summary.sh and confirmed .ralph/current_tasks.md is still empty.
- Next I should validate the repository with make check, make test, and make lint as required when no active tasks remain.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries again and confirmed there are no active tasks left in .ralph/current_tasks.md.
- Validated the actual repository state: make check passed, make test passed across the Go packages, and make lint passed.
- Next after reopening: if no new tasks are added, the migration is complete for now; .ralph/STOP should exist and there is no task to select.

- Read prior progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, and re-read current task selection rules.
- Found one active task in .ralph/current_tasks.md: story-01 foundation task-04, with blocker task-03 already marked passes=true in .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, so it is now unblocked and selectable.
- Next: confirm the repository stack from codebase shape, write the chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt, and quit immediately.

- Confirmed the actual repository stack is a Go 1.26.2 service (`cmd/pg_gobench` plus `internal/*`) with Make targets for lint/test via gofmt, go vet, and go test.
- Selected the only active unblocked failing task: .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param.md. Its blocker task-03 is already passes=true in .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Next after reopening: execute .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param.md, then re-run make check, make test, and make lint to keep the chain green.

- Read prior codex progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param.md and I am now reading the task file to determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution.
- Next: inspect the task body and any linked plan, then either create/update the plan and quit or execute the plan with TDD.

- Read the active task body and confirmed it was still in the planning branch, so I did not execute code changes.
- Inspected internal/config, internal/database, README, and the example configs; identified the current boundary smell as adapter-side sslmode derivation from TLS path presence.
- Added .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param_plans/2026-04-30-sslmode-config-plan.md with a TDD-first execution plan that moves sslmode validation into internal/config, removes database-layer inference, and updates docs/examples; linked that plan from the task file and ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Next after reopening: review the plan, and once the design is considered sound replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and quit immediately, or execute only after that state change per Ralph workflow.

- Read the previous progress log, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill bodies.
- Confirmed the active task is story-01 foundation task-04 and I am now reading the task file plus its linked plan to decide whether this turn is planning-only or execution.
- Next: inspect the task status marker and plan tail; if it still says TO BE VERIFIED I must stay in planning mode and quit after any needed plan update.

- Read the active task, linked plan, and the relevant internal/config and internal/database code plus existing tests to verify the planned design.
- Confirmed the boundary smell is real: database.Open still derives sslmode from TLS path presence, while cert/key pairing is validated in the adapter instead of config loading.
- Promoted .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param_plans/2026-04-30-sslmode-config-plan.md from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE. Next turn should execute that plan with strict red-green TDD slices, then run make check, make lint, and make test.

- Read the previous codex progress, current task pointer, and the improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill bodies again for this turn.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-01-foundation/task-04-sslmode-config-param.md and I am now reading the task body and linked plan to execute only if the plan still ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: inspect the task checklist and plan details, then implement in strict red-green slices and keep appending progress as I go.

- Verified the linked plan still ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is the implementation turn rather than another planning-only turn.
- Read the plan and started inspecting internal/config, internal/database, README, and runnable example configs to prepare the first failing TDD slice around explicit source.sslmode.
- Next: add the first red tests for config loading and adapter sslmode pass-through, then implement the minimal config/database changes to make them pass.

- Added TDD slices for valid source.sslmode loading, invalid/mapping-shaped/literal-only sslmode rejection, adapter sslmode pass-through, and incompatible TLS plus partial client-cert/key validation.
- Moved sslmode ownership into internal/config with a new SSLMode type and config-side TLS compatibility validation; updated internal/database to pass the configured sslmode directly instead of deriving it.
- Next: update all existing config fixtures/tests/docs/examples to include required source.sslmode, remove remaining adapter-side duplication, then run package tests followed by make check/lint/test.

- Updated the existing config/app/database fixtures and runnable example configs to include explicit source.sslmode, and corrected one env-literal test fixture so it still exercises literal handling under the new TLS compatibility rules.
- Relevant package suites are green: go test ./internal/config ./internal/database ./internal/app passed after removing adapter-side sslmode inference and moving TLS compatibility checks into config loading.
- Next: finish repo-level validation with make check, make lint, and make test; then update the Ralph task status and completion bookkeeping if the full gates stay green.

- Full repo gates are green: make check passed, make lint passed, and make test passed after the explicit source.sslmode refactor and fixture/doc updates.
- Marked task-04 and its execution plan complete in .ralph, and I am now running .ralph/task_switch.sh before the required final commit and push.
- Next: inspect the post-switch git state, stage all files including .ralph bookkeeping, commit with the required task-finished format including test evidence and implementation notes, push, then stop immediately.
